title: Golden Brown Irish Minced Beef Hash
description: A savory Irish dish featuring minced beef, perfect for a brunch mood, with a dairy-free friendly twist and a rich umami flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Boil the diced potatoes in salted water until they are slightly tender, then drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat and cook the minced beef until it is browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the boiled potatoes, salt, black pepper, and paprika to the skillet and stir to combine.
Use the beef broth to deglaze the skillet, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
